data.title: Tesla reported strong Q4 results and plans to invest $2 billion into CEO Elon Musk's AI startup xAI. Lockheed Martin is expected to report earnings at $5.74/share on revenue of $19.85 billion.
data.description: Today, U.S. stock futures are up and investors are eyeing certain stocks. Lockheed Martin Corp. (NYSE: LMT) is expected to announce earnings of $5.74 per share on revenue of $19.85 billion. In after-hours trading, the company's shares fell by 0.2% to $596.16. On the other hand, Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) reported strong results for the fourth quarter. Additionally, Tesla will be investing around $2 billion in CEO Elon Musk's artificial intelligence startup, xAI. The company is preparing for production ramps of Tesla Semi in North America. Check out the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Robbyant Open-Sources LingBot-World, a top-performing world model for real-time interaction; ideal for AI and gaming.
data.description: In Shanghai, Robbyant, an AI company under Ant Group, has unveiled LingBot-World, an open-source world model which excels in video quality, dynamic fidelity, long-term consistency, and interactivity. This cutting-edge technology is particularly tailored for embodied intelligence, autonomous driving, and game development. Offering a high degree of fidelity and dynamism, LingBot-World serves as a real-time controllable "digital sandbox" for simulation and training purposes, setting a new industry standard in performance metrics.

data.title: ETH exchange supply decreases due to rising staking while price remains steady. 3.6M ETH in queue for validation with a 63-day wait.
data.description: The Ethereum network is currently experiencing significant congestion in the validator entry queue, with a staggering 3.6 million ETH awaiting staking. It is predicted that individuals looking to stake their ETH will be required to wait approximately 63 days before their transactions are processed.

data.title: Co-founder and board of Cere Network hit with $100M lawsuit for alleged fraud in 2021 token sale.
data.description: For the second time this month, Cere Network is under legal scrutiny as a new lawsuit claims fraud by its co-founder and board regarding a public token launch in 2021. This is another setback for the platform, which has already been sued once this month. The lawsuit alleges fraudulent activities in relation to the token offering, with specific details from the launch year. This legal action adds to the existing challenges for Cere Network, raising concerns about the company's practices. Interestingly, this is the second lawsuit filed against the platform in a short span, highlighting the severity of the accusations. The lawsuit involves significant allegations of fraud, putting the spotlight on the co-founder and board members.

data.title: Virtualware appoints Stephanie Smith, P. Eng., former CANDU Energy Chief Nuclear Engineer, to its Advisory Board.
data.description: Virtualware, a leading company in 3D software, has welcomed Stephanie Smith, P. Eng., a distinguished Canadian nuclear engineer, to its global advisory board.

data.title: China's personal luxury market shrinks by 3%-5% in 2025 but shows signs of recovery, less decline than in 2024.
data.description: In 2025, the personal luxury goods market in mainland China experienced a contraction of 3%–5%, indicating a notable slowdown from the sharp decline observed in 2024, as per Bain & Company's China Personal Luxury Report. This data highlights a more moderate negative trend in the market. Despite the challenges, consumer confidence in the luxury segment...

data.title: Gentoo Media reports Q4 2025 update, previews 2026 guidance, and starts bond refinancing process.
data.description: In Birkirkara, Malta, on January 29, 2026, Gentoo Media Inc. unveiled its trading report for Q4 of 2025, offering an initial financial outlook for 2026. They also revealed plans for a refinancing initiative. The company aims to enhance its financial position in the foreseeable future.

data.title: NBPE announces a transaction in own shares, restricted in certain countries. Not for release in Australia, Canada, Italy, Denmark, Japan, US.
data.description: The data provided in this document should not be disclosed, published, or distributed in Australia, Canada, Italy, Denmark, Japan, the United States, or to any citizens of these regions.

data.title: ABN AMRO appoints Michiel Lap as Chair and nominates Jean-Pierre Mustier to its Supervisory Board.
data.description: ABN AMRO has announced the appointment of Michiel Lap as the Chair of the Supervisory Board. Additionally, the bank has nominated Jean-Pierre Mustier to join the board as a new member. Lap will be leading the board with his extensive experience in the industry. ABN AMRO's decision to bring on Mustier reflects their strategic approach to strengthening the board with diverse talents. The move aims to enhance governance and leadership within the bank. These appointments come as ABN AMRO continues to focus on refining its board composition, with Lap and Mustier poised to play integral roles in driving the bank's strategic direction.

data.title: Endeavour meets guidance with 1,209koz production at ~$1,435/oz AISC for FY-2025 and declares record H2-2025 dividend of $200m amidst >$1bn shareholder returns program.
data.description: Endeavour successfully meets projected targets and announces a historic dividend payment for the second half of 2025. Producing 1,209 thousand ounces in the fiscal year of 2025 at an all-in sustaining cost of approximately $1,435 per ounce. The dividend for the second half of 2025 amounts to $200 million, marking a significant milestone. Furthermore, the company has initiated a shareholder returns program exceeding $1 billion in value.

data.title: Embassy of United Roma Tribes established in The Hague on January 21, 2026, for diplomatic relations.
data.description: In The Hague, Netherlands, on January 21, 2026, the Embassy of the United Roma Tribes was officially founded through a notarial deed, with its headquarters located in The Hague. This Embassy has been set up to facilitate cultural, social, economic, and humanitarian collaboration.

data.title: Swiss bank Sygnum collects more than 750 BTC for market-neutral fund, with 8.9% Q4 returns.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, Sygnum reported that its market-neutral BTC Alpha Fund achieved annualized returns of 8.9%. The fund successfully raised over 750 Bitcoin from a group of professional and institutional investors.
